steps: Find the sample mean. Add the five tree counts: \(51 + 59 + 45 + 52 + 73 = 280\). Divide by 5: \(280 \div 5 = 56\) trees per acre.
Scale to the full forest: \(56 \times 253\). Break it apart: \(56 \times 250 = 14{,}000\) and \(56 \times 3 = 168\). Total estimate = \(14{,}168\) trees.
Identify which answer range contains \(14{,}168\): the interval 13,500 to 14,500 does.

• Choice A: 11,000 to 12,000 — the estimate 14,168 is above this range; incorrect.
  • Choice B: 12,500 to 13,500 — 14,168 exceeds 13,500; incorrect.
  • Choice C: 13,500 to 14,500 — 14,168 falls within this range. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • Choice D: 18,000 to 19,000 — this would require a mean of about 71 trees per acre, well above the sample mean of 56; incorrect.
